validation.note: Real-curated validation fixture. Held-out future evidence stays separate from event-time replay inputs.

rows.expected_observable: Data Center revenue should remain the dominant revenue driver in the next reported quarter.
rows.label: validated
rows.what_happened: Held-out Q2 fiscal 2025 results reported revenue of $30.0 billion and Data Center revenue of $26.3 billion, up 16% from Q1, keeping Data Center demand central to the follow-up evidence.

rows.expected_observable: The next reported quarter should land near or above the guided revenue range.
rows.label: pending
rows.what_happened: Pending separate curation of the guidance-accuracy label; held-out Q2 results are available for review.

rows.expected_observable: Gaming revenue should become a central explanation in follow-up commentary.
rows.label: pending
rows.what_happened: Pending; no held-out validation label has been assigned to the gaming-rebound narrative.

rows.expected_observable: Later commentary should focus on Blackwell demand and production timing.
rows.label: pending
rows.what_happened: Pending separate curation of Blackwell-specific validation; held-out Q2 commentary references Hopper demand and Blackwell anticipation.

validation.note: Real-curated validation fixture. Held-out future evidence stays separate from event-time replay inputs.

rows.expected_observable: Later reported results should show revenue pressure extending beyond the fourth-quarter release.
rows.label: validated
rows.what_happened: Held-out Q1 fiscal 2025 results reported revenue down 10% reported and NIKE Direct revenue down 13%, supporting the replay-time fiscal 2025 demand reset narrative.

rows.expected_observable: Future results should keep NIKE Direct and digital revenue declines central.
rows.label: pending
rows.what_happened: Held-out Q1 results showed NIKE Direct revenue down 13%, but the public validation label remains pending because the broader demand reset better explains the event-time move.

rows.expected_observable: Future commentary should cite Greater China or macro uncertainty as the main driver.
rows.label: pending
rows.what_happened: Pending separate curation of the geographic and macro contribution; held-out Q1 results showed declines across geographies but the replay winner was broader.

rows.expected_observable: Follow-up discussion should emphasize margin expansion and EPS resilience over demand weakness.
rows.label: invalidated
rows.what_happened: Held-out Q1 results still showed gross-margin improvement, but revenue, Direct, wholesale, and EPS declined, weakening a margin-led explanation for the original selloff.

validation.note: Real-curated validation fixture. Held-out future evidence stays separate from event-time replay inputs.

rows.expected_observable: Post-lock technical evidence should confirm a CrowdStrike-controlled update mechanism rather than an outside attack.
rows.label: validated
rows.what_happened: Held-out CrowdStrike technical details identified a Windows sensor configuration update and logic error, while the later Q2 release referenced customer recovery work and Channel File 291 incident costs, supporting the operational reliability liability narrative.

rows.expected_observable: Later technical disclosures would need to identify malicious activity or breach mechanics.
rows.label: invalidated
rows.what_happened: Held-out technical details kept the cause inside CrowdStrike's update path and stated the issue was not related to a cyberattack, weakening a cyberattack-scare explanation.

rows.expected_observable: Later evidence should continue to emphasize Windows-host scope and Microsoft ecosystem exposure.
rows.label: pending
rows.what_happened: Held-out technical details confirmed Windows-host scope, but the validation label remains pending because the financial validation evidence centered more directly on CrowdStrike customer recovery and incident costs.

rows.expected_observable: Future company results should show limited customer, ARR, guidance, or incident-cost effects.
rows.label: invalidated
rows.what_happened: Held-out Q2 fiscal 2025 results referenced customer recovery work and Channel File 291 incident related costs, reducing confidence that the original selloff was only a temporary outage overreaction.

cases.label: SAVE January 2024 JetBlue merger block real-curated replay

validation.note: Real-curated validation fixture. Held-out future evidence stays separate from event-time replay inputs.

rows.expected_observable: Held-out validation should show the merger agreement terminated rather than merely delayed.
rows.label: validated
rows.what_happened: Held-out March 4 company and news evidence showed JetBlue and Spirit terminated the merger agreement because regulatory obstacles made timely closing unlikely, validating merger optionality collapse as the replay winner.

rows.expected_observable: Future disclosures should emphasize refinancing, liquidity, or profitability actions.
rows.label: partial
rows.what_happened: Spirit did discuss standalone initiatives, refinancing, and profitability steps, but the validation source was still anchored to termination of the merger path.

rows.expected_observable: The narrative should weaken if the companies terminate the agreement because closing conditions cannot be met.
rows.label: invalidated
rows.what_happened: The companies terminated the agreement rather than preserving a live appeal-led deal path, weakening the appeal optionality explanation.

rows.expected_observable: Other airline merger targets or peers should show similar abnormal pressure if this narrative dominates.
rows.label: pending
rows.what_happened: The validation fixture keeps broader airline regulatory read-through pending because the held-out evidence was specific to JetBlue and Spirit.

cases.label: MMM August 2023 Combat Arms settlement real-curated replay

validation.note: Real-curated validation fixture. Held-out future evidence stays separate from event-time replay inputs.

rows.expected_observable: Future company evidence should show high claimant participation or settlement implementation progress.
rows.label: validated
rows.what_happened: Held-out 3M evidence said claimant participation was on track to exceed the 98% threshold and later filing language preserved the settlement structure, validating litigation overhang compression as the replay winner.

rows.expected_observable: Future validation should center PFAS or other liabilities rather than earplug settlement progress if this narrative wins.
rows.label: partial
rows.what_happened: Residual legal exposure remained relevant, but the held-out validation evidence directly centered Combat Arms participation and settlement implementation.

rows.expected_observable: The burden narrative should weaken if future company evidence confirms participation progress and cash election clarity.
rows.label: invalidated
rows.what_happened: 3M later said participation was on track to exceed the threshold and elected cash for the stock component, weakening the idea that settlement burden was the primary event narrative.

rows.expected_observable: Future validation should cite litigation posture rather than participation milestones if this narrative wins.
rows.label: pending
rows.what_happened: The no-liability posture remained part of the case file but did not become the central held-out validation evidence.
